subject: UX & Research
title: Hub and Spoke Navigation
description: One-minute skill: Hub and Spoke Navigation.

hook.text: The iPhone home screen is a hub. Tap an app, go deep into it, press home, return to the hub. Hub and spoke.

buildup.text: Hub and spoke keeps the architecture simple: everything connects to the center, but not to each other.

discovery.text: This pattern works for apps with distinct, unrelated sections. Each spoke is independent and self-contained.

twist.text: The downside: moving between spokes requires returning to the hub first. Cross-section navigation is slow.

climax.text: Tab bars solve the cross-section problem: they're a persistent hub on every screen. Instagram and Twitter use this.

punchline.text: Hub and spoke is simple. Add a persistent tab bar for speed.

quiz.question: What is the main limitation of hub-and-spoke navigation?
options: Users must return to the hub to switch between unrelated sections, slowing cross-section movement
It only works on mobile devices
Users can't navigate more than two levels deep
